items.title: Powering the World in the Age of #AI #tech
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Patrick Maloney, Chairman & Co-Founder, The Nuclear Co.; Emily McAteer, Co-Founder & CEO, Odyssey Energy Solutions and Josephine Wapakabulo, Founder & Managing Director, TIG Africa discuss how rising AI and energy demand heightens global power and climate challenges with Bloomberg's John Fraher at the 2025 Bloomberg New Economy Forum in Singapore.
--------

items.title: Goldman's Waldron on how tariffs are impacting the economy
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"We believe that much of the absorption of tariffs has actually been in the value chain in the company margin and hasn't been passed on," says Goldman Sachs President and COO John Waldron at the Bloomberg #NewEconomyForum in Singapore.

Follow the latest here: http://bloom.bg/44g7i9G</article>

items.title: Catch Hospitality's Remm on the Future of Dining as He Opens New Concept "The Eighty Six"
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Eugene Rem, Co-Founder and Partner of Catch Hospitality Group, discusses New York’s cocktail culture, the impact of GLP-1 use on restaurants, and the group’s new concept “The Eighty Six” with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close.” He says his team seeks iconic, smaller locations to create a more intimate dining experience.
--------

items.title: Inside America’s Hidden Capitals of Generosity
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>From small-town Michigan to Walmart’s hometown, we visit the most charitable places in America. Former Blackstone vice chair Tony James, Arkansas philanthropist Aaron Marshall and scholar Kevin Fitzpatrick share what they’ve learned about the “return on investment” from sharing time, money and kindness.
--------

items.title: What Saudi Arabia’s deepening US ties mean for investors #shorts #saudiarabia #investing #markets
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Saudi Arabia’s Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman promises to bring as much as $1 trillion in investment to the US, as the country tries to pivot away from oil towards becoming a global technology and investment player. Tune in at 6 p.m. ET bloom.bg/48fzN8N</article>

items.title: Wall Street Week | Saudi Foreign Investment, Charitable Places, A Market for All Energy, Zak Brown
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>This week, Saudi Arabia’s Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman promises to bring as much as $1 trillion in investment to the US as Saudi Arabia tries to pivot from oil towards becoming a global technology and investment player. And, from small-town Michigan to the headquarters of Walmart, we reveal how philanthropy, volunteering and community ties power America’s hidden generosity. Plus, what’s driving the US’s power grid strain? Later, McLaren’s Zak Brown reveals what it really takes to build a winning culture and why great leaders never get comfortable at the top.

Chapters:

00:10:45 - America’s Most Charitable Places
00:23:48 - Winning Combination of Energy
00:34:20 - McLaren’s Zak Brown
00:47:26 - PBS Extra: Deborah Lehr on Saudi Arabia

items.title: Inside the MIT Lab Building The Future of Bionic Limbs
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Inside the MIT Lab Building The Future of Bionic Limbs 

Hugh Herr, professor of media arts and sciences at the MIT Media Lab, says that his experience losing both of his lower legs at age 17 drove him to expand the capability of prosthetic limbs to respond to the human nervous system and sensory input. Herr says that the lab's goal for the coming decades is to connect the human brain to prosthesis and potentially restore movement to limbs that have lost the ability. 
--------
Watch Bloomberg Radio LIVE on YouTube
Weekdays 7am-6pm ET

items.title: Africa's Shifting Investment Landscape: Risks and Opportunities | Bloomberg Next Africa
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>In this episode of Next Africa, we take you inside the inaugural Bloomberg Africa Business Summit, where influential leaders, investors, and innovators including South African billionaire Patrice Motsepe, Sygnia founder Magda Wierzycka and JPMorgan's Daniel Pinto gathered to discuss the forces reshaping the continent’s economic future. From policy shifts and technology breakthroughs to sustainability, finance, and trade partnerships, we bring you key insights and exclusive conversations with the people driving Africa’s next era of growth. With one of the world’s youngest populations, abundant natural resources, and deepening trade and investment ties, Africa is emerging as a key player in shaping the world’s economic and innovation landscape. Bloomberg's Next Africa is hosted by Jennifer Zabasajja.

00:00 Intro and show open
01:11 Investor's guide to Africa
04:39 Patrice Motsepe on Africa's private sector
06:36 Emrie Brown on transforming Africa's growth engines
07:48 Egypt's Hassan El Khatib on new trade horizons
09:25 Benjamin Hung on South-South trade boom
10:40 Ronald Lamola on rethinking trade policies
12:49 Magda Wierzycka on Africa's youth potential
17:02 PIC's Patrick Dlamini on Africa's youth potential
18:00 JPMorgan's Daniel Pinto on Africa's economic landscape
19:05 Owen Omogiafo on opportunities in Africa
19:56 Jeremy Awori on Africa's moment to leapfrog  
21:40 Richard Stewart on Africa's critical minerals boom
22:46 Samaila Zubairu on demand for Africa's critical minerals
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is holding even if the situation remains fragile, says US Ambassador to the UN, Mike Waltz, in defense of a US-backed peace plan amid continued violence in Gaza.</article>

items.title: Standard Chartered's big bet on digital assets
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Standard Chartered bank CFO Diego De Giorgi says digital assets like stablecoins and cryptocurrencies may 'change the payment landscape of the world.' In July, StanChart became the first major bank to facilitate spot trading in crypto for institutional customers. On Bloomberg Chief Future Officer, De Giorgi and CEO Bill Winters tell Anna Edwards why they're setting the bank on a course to support clients' access to digital assets and payment mechanisms.</article>

items.title: Trump Embraces Mamdani During First Meet in Oval Office | Balance of Power 11/21/2025
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Melinda Haring, Non-Resident Senior Fellow at the Atlantic Council's Eurasia Center, voices her frustrations with the Ukraine peace plan created by President Trump. Rep. Glenn Ivey (D-MD) discusses President Trump embracing NYC Mayor-elect Zohran Mamdani during their meeting in the White House.

Chapters:

items.title: Trump, Mamdani find common ground in their goals for NYC#shorts #mamdani #trump #politics #nyc
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>President Donald Trump said he wants to see Zohran Mamdani succeed, saying after a highly anticipated sitdown that he’d “feel very comfortable” living in a New York run by the mayor-elect and that he doesn’t anticipate cutting off funding for the country’s most populous city. Myles Miller reports https://bloom.bg/48sAZaa</article>

items.title: Inside MBS’s Push to Shift Saudi Arabia’s Oil Wealth to Tech
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>After President Trump gave a royal welcome to Saudi Arabia’s Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, the Kingdom’s strategic shift from petrochemicals to technology is expected to materialize in the form of investment. We break down what investors can expect with Basilinna’s Deborah Lehr and Council on Foreign Relations’ Steven Cook.
--------

items.title: Stocks Steady After Volatile Week as Bitcoin ETFs See Major Outflows | The Close 11/21/2025
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Morgan Stanley’s Michelle Weaver, Loop Capital Markets’ Anthony Chukumba, Cambridge Associates’ Andrea Auerbach, JP Morgan Asset Management’s Meera Pandit, Centerbridge Partners’ Jeff Aronson, Catch Hospitality Group’s Eugene Remm, Levain Bakery’s Connie McDonald and Pam Weekes.
